purpose: Canonical New Alphabet web runtime for editorial and workspace surfaces.
required_bindings: Leptos owns routing, shell composition, and hydration-safe state.
New Alphabet foundations emit the stylesheet and token law.
Recipes remain the first choice for surface geometry.
laws: The constitution stays primary and hydration must not change layout meaning.
Semantic HTML stays visible rather than disappearing behind opaque wrappers.
First builds keep the canonical New Alphabet palette strict.
Entity surfaces and controls use the subtle radius token while the background grid remains sharp.
anti_patterns: Hydration-only layout shifts.
Router chrome that displaces the primary work surface.
Runtime palette improvisation or theme-family sprawl at V0.

purpose: Dense desktop analytics binding for table-first work surfaces with native Rust services.
required_bindings: Dioxus owns the shell, pane layout, and local interaction state.
Polars owns filtering, shaping, grouping, aggregation, sorting, and export slices.
Charton owns chart grammar, scales, legends, and SVG rendering output.
laws: The application is a workbench, not a dashboard mural or marketing page.
Query state is shared so table, chart, summary metrics, and inspector derive from the same Rust state.
Charts live in bounded panes and stay subordinate to the table or list.
Use left rail, compact top controls, central work surface, and right analysis pane before inventing new geometry.
First builds keep the canonical New Alphabet palette strict and later adjustments may only remap semantic color roles.
Corners use the subtle radius token across panes, controls, and chart containers while the background grid remains sharp.
anti_patterns: Hand-drawn chart primitives in Dioxus when Charton can express the chart.
Full-window charts that push the primary table below the fold.
Oversized cards, welcome states, or web-marketing headers on data screens.

purpose: Desktop workbench binding for a Leptos frontend in Tauri with native Rust data commands.
required_bindings: Tauri owns the native shell and command boundary.
Leptos owns shell layout, interaction, and direct SVG embedding.
Polars and Charton live in native Rust commands or native state rather than frontend WASM.
The frontend requests shaped table slices, chart SVG, and optional chart spec JSON instead of raw datasets.
laws: Heavy aggregation stays in Rust and never migrates into the frontend view layer.
Use CSS grid for shell geometry with sidebar width 248, toolbar height 36, main surface minmax(0, 1fr), and inspector width 300 when the inspector is present.
Chart transport uses SVG first, Vega-Lite JSON second, and PNG only for export or fallback.
Charts embed directly in bounded panes, preserve viewBox, use explicit compact height, and never push filters or toolbar controls below the fold.
Command boundaries stay coarse: one command for query refresh, one for chart refresh, and one for export.
The table remains the anchor surface and shows real work at 1440x900.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp.
The Tauri shell plus Leptos frontend boundary is exact, so no additional host tolerance is required here.
anti_patterns: Heavy aggregation or schema shaping in frontend WASM.
Dozens of chatty Tauri command calls for one user action.
Ad hoc nested flex shells that weaken the workbench hierarchy.
Charts that dominate the window or collapse the table below the fold.

purpose: Desktop workbench binding for a Yew frontend in Tauri with native Rust data commands.
required_bindings: Tauri owns the native shell and command boundary.
Yew owns shell layout, interaction, and direct SVG embedding.
Polars and Charton live in native Rust commands or native state rather than frontend WASM.
The frontend requests shaped table slices, chart SVG, and optional chart spec JSON instead of raw datasets.
laws: Heavy aggregation stays in Rust and never migrates into the frontend view layer.
Use CSS grid for shell geometry with sidebar width 248, toolbar height 36, main surface minmax(0, 1fr), and inspector width 300 when the inspector is present.
Chart transport uses SVG first, Vega-Lite JSON second, and PNG only for export or fallback.
Charts embed directly in bounded panes, preserve viewBox, use explicit compact height, and never push filters or toolbar controls below the fold.
Command boundaries stay coarse: one command for query refresh, one for chart refresh, and one for export.
The table remains the anchor surface and shows real work at 1440x900.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp.
The Tauri shell plus Yew frontend boundary is exact, so no additional host tolerance is required here.
anti_patterns: Heavy aggregation or schema shaping in frontend WASM.
Dozens of chatty Tauri command calls for one user action.
Ad hoc nested flex shells that weaken the workbench hierarchy.
Charts that dominate the window or collapse the table below the fold.

purpose: Dense desktop workbench binding for Floem with disciplined stacks, wrapper restraint, and native Rust data services.
required_bindings: Floem owns the shell through h_stack and v_stack composition rather than free layout invention.
One root style layer sets shared density and typography and child regions override it rarely.
Polars owns file reads, lazy queries, joins, filters, aggregations, table slices, and export-ready datasets.
Charton owns chart grammar, theming, axis policy, legend policy, and SVG-first output.
laws: Dense shell bindings use body 13, meta 12, section title 16, page title 20, control height 32, toolbar height 36, sidebar width 240, inspector width 300, gap x 8, gap y 6, and panel padding 8.
Use container only for a real style boundary, clip boundary, or region identity and report wrapper count per major region.
Use virtual_stack for long lists and keep scrolling inside the main content region.
Rows should read as desktop rows rather than mobile cards and the first render must show real work instead of decorative empty space.
The table remains primary, the chart remains explanatory, and no chart should push the working table below the fold.
Values above 12 are invalid in the default dense shell; Floem uses 8 for shell rhythm and 6 for dense vertical rhythm.
Business aggregation never lives in the view layer and chart geometry is never hand-assembled when Charton can express it declaratively.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp.
anti_patterns: Casual container wrappers that add padding without region identity.
Marketing-card layouts, giant headers, or decorative empty regions.
Heavy aggregation or chart logic in the Floem view layer.
Global scrolling that weakens the fixed workbench shell.

purpose: Dense desktop tool binding for GPUI with disciplined pane structure, compact controls, and work-first surfaces.
required_bindings: GPUI owns the application shell through horizontal and vertical flex composition rather than freeform canvas behavior.
The shell keeps a fixed left navigation width, a flexible center pane, an optional fixed right inspector, and one compact top toolbar row.
Polars owns file reads, lazy queries, joins, filters, aggregations, table slices, and export-ready datasets.
Charton owns chart grammar, theming, axis policy, legend policy, and SVG-first output.
laws: Default text uses 13px, page titles stay at or below 20px, and compact controls use 32px height.
GPUI uses 8 for structural padding, 8 for shell gaps, and 6 only inside dense local control groups; 4 is reserved for inline micro-spacing.
Scrolling lives in the content region rather than across the whole shell.
Prefer virtualized list or table components for dense datasets and report virtualization usage in self-audit.
Prefer dock and split-pane patterns for multi-surface tools and simple surfaces over card piles.
Leaf controls shrink to content or fixed compact sizes and full-size leaf widgets appear only when the leaf is the real editor or table.
The center pane must show useful content immediately at 1440x900.
The 8 and 6 split exists because GPUI dock surfaces and local tool groups need two exact rhythms rather than one approximate spacing band.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp.
anti_patterns: Freeform canvas composition that weakens structured pane hierarchy.
Giant panes with one tiny child.
Repeated banner alerts, oversized iconography, or decorative chrome that steals vertical space.
Nested padding on every wrapper or giant flex children used as decorative whitespace.

purpose: Native desktop workbench binding for Relm4 with GTK structure, compact density, and serious data surfaces.
required_bindings: Use ApplicationWindow as the root shell and a compact HeaderBar or top toolbar as the primary action row.
Use Paned for two- or three-pane shells and ScrolledWindow around main content regions.
Use ListView or ColumnView for datasets, Stack or StackSwitcher for major modes, and dialogs or popovers only for secondary actions.
Polars owns file reads, lazy queries, joins, filters, aggregations, table slices, and export-ready datasets.
Charton owns chart grammar, theming, axis policy, legend policy, and SVG-first output.
laws: Sidebar width-request stays between 232 and 256 and inspector width-request stays between 280 and 320.
GTK spacing uses 8 for shell and panel structure and 6 for dense control rhythm.
Assign css-classes to all major regions and theme with GTK CSS instead of per-widget styling hacks.
Use ColumnView for multi-column data rather than nested boxes pretending to be a table.
Do not nest Box inside Box inside Box to fake grid structure and do not rely on card-heavy framing.
Dense rows ellipsize long labels, keep row height stable, and avoid unexpected wrapping.
Primary actions stay visible without scrolling and scrolling remains local to the main content region.
Use subtle borders or separators where useful and avoid giant title blocks or oversized empty states.
The width-request bands remain narrow ranges because GTK and libadwaita widget metrics vary slightly across native control rendering.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp when CSS rounding is applied.
anti_patterns: Nested box hierarchies used as fake tables or grids.
Wrapped row labels that expand dense list height unexpectedly.
Card-heavy framing, oversized empty states, or decorative chrome that weakens the native workbench feel.
Per-widget style hacks that bypass shared GTK CSS classes.

purpose: Dense desktop workbench binding for Slint with explicit layout law, constrained pane sizing, and token-driven shell structure.
required_bindings: Use Slint layouts for all structure and never build the shell with loose Rectangle primitives.
Set Window.default-font-size explicitly and use logical px consistently throughout the surface.
Set spacing and padding explicitly on every layout that establishes shell or panel structure.
Use preferred-width or preferred-height for sidebar, toolbar, and inspector regions and use min or max widths when panes must stay legible.
laws: Do not use percentage widths except at the top shell level where the application frame is being divided.
Favor a HorizontalLayout and VerticalLayout shell before introducing GridLayout for dense forms, metadata panels, or inspector regions.
Put TextInput only inside constrained layouts so fields never float in unconstrained space.
Use GridLayout for dense forms and metadata panels rather than improvised row stacks.
Shell composition should read as a serious desktop workbench rather than decorative scene graph construction.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp where Slint styling applies rounding.
anti_patterns: Loose Rectangle shells used as structural layout.
Implicit spacing or padding in structural layouts.
Percentage-based pane sizing below the top shell level.
Unconstrained TextInput fields or improvised dense forms built without GridLayout.

purpose: Dense desktop workbench binding for egui with explicit compact style installation, panel shell structure, and restrained framing.
required_bindings: Emit install_compact_style(ctx) before any screen code so compact New Alphabet density is installed first.
Set text_styles explicitly for Heading, Body, Button, and Small.
Set spacing.item_spacing, spacing.button_padding, spacing.interact_size.y, and spacing.window_margin explicitly.
Use SidePanel, TopBottomPanel, and CentralPanel for the shell and keep scrolling inside central content only.
laws: Avoid nested Frame::group calls and do not use more than one framed wrapper around routine content.
Use ui.add_space only at 4.0 or 8.0 and prefer 0.0 when layout containers already carry the rhythm.
Prefer Grid for dense label-value regions and compact form layouts.
Keep the shell panel-first and do not let local framed wrappers become the page hierarchy.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp where egui styling applies rounding.
anti_patterns: Screen code emitted before install_compact_style(ctx).
Implicit egui text styles or spacing defaults standing in for explicit density law.
Nested Frame::group wrappers around routine content.
Scrolling outside the central content region or dense forms built without Grid.

purpose: Dense desktop workbench binding for Iced with strict fill discipline, bounded pane sizing, and table-first shell structure.
required_bindings: Length::Fill is allowed only on the app root container, the main content pane, scrollable regions, and first-level split panes.
Length::FillPortion is allowed only on the first split between major panes.
Buttons, labels, chips, form rows, and tool groups use Shrink or Fixed sizing instead of Fill behavior.
Prefer Scrollable only around the main body and never around the whole application shell.
laws: Sidebar width stays between Fixed(232.0) and Fixed(260.0) and inspector width stays between Fixed(280.0) and Fixed(340.0).
Toolbar height stays between Fixed(34.0) and Fixed(38.0).
Row and Column spacing uses 8 for structural rhythm and 4 only inside dense inline control groups.
Row and Column padding uses 8 outside the root shell and does not exceed 8.
Never nest more than two padded containers.
Prefer table or list rows over cards so routine content stays dense and legible.
The width bands remain narrow ranges because Iced split panes and content tables need a small tolerance band to stay legible across window states.
Corners use the subtle radius token on entity surfaces and controls while the background grid remains sharp where Iced styling applies rounding.
anti_patterns: Length::Fill applied to buttons, labels, chips, form rows, or local tool groups.
Length::FillPortion used below the first split between major panes.
Scrollable wrapped around the entire app shell.
Card-first routine content or padding stacks deeper than two containers.
